title: rubus lanceolatus
intro: Rubus lanceolatus, also known as the Lance-leaved Bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the British Isles. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus lanceolatus include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and protect against cell damage. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations are used to support overall well-being.
